channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Utah senators react to contentious Oval Office meeting between Trump and Zelenskyy
item.description: Utah's senators are responding to a contentious interaction between Trump and Vance and Zelenskyy Friday with contrasting public statements.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, ABC 4

item.title: JD Vance gets his long-awaited moment to admonish Ukraine's Zelenskyy
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Vice President JD Vance was dismissing Ukraine long before he upended an Oval Office meeting Friday by calling Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy “disrespectful” and asking if he had ever thanked the U.S. for its support. When Vance was a candidate for U.S.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Iowa's governor signs a bill removing gender identity protections from the state’s civil rights code
item.description: Iowa has became the first U.S. state to remove gender identity protections from its civil rights code.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where things stand as Congress tries to avoid a partial government shutdown in two weeks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As House members finished voting for the week and left Washington, the lead Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee, Rep.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Law school project finds slavery citations still being used today
item.description: EAST LANSING, Mich. (AP) — An 1842 U.S. Supreme Court ruling overturning the kidnapping conviction of a white man who seized a Black family and forced them into slavery south of the Mason-Dixon line is still being cited in American jurisprudence, 160 years after enslaved people throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump, Vance go off on Zelensky in contentious Oval Office spat
item.description: President Trump went off on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during an extraordinary Oval Office meeting in front of cameras on Friday in which he raised his voice and called the foreign leader “disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Best mullet? Best use of Taylor Swift? AP hands out its own movie awards ahead of the Oscars
item.description: Hollywood's never-ending awards season can make it easy to get a little lost in the run-of-the-mill “best” categories. Actor. Actress. Director. Picture. A great movie can be reduced to a single performance; those that are left out seem to simply vanish for a while.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: I-40 to reopen at Tennessee-North Carolina state line
item.description: The four-mile stretch of I-40 on the Tennessee and North Carolina border will reopen to motor traffic Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Hillmer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he plans to add White House ballroom
item.description: President Trump in a new interview said he is planning to pave part of the Rose Garden, similar to a patio at his Mar-a-Lago property in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Man charged after allegedly faking seizure as puppies were stolen
item.description: A 37-year-old man has been formally charged for his alleged role in the theft of two bulldogs from a Centennial pet store.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, NewsNation

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife tested negative for carbon monoxide, sheriff says
item.description: Actor Gene Hackman, his wife Betsy Arakawa, and their dog were found dead in their Santa Fe home, in a scene that one detective had deemed "suspicious enough in nature to require a thorough search and investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, KRON4

item.title: Commissioner Ford Jr. charged with bribery, tax evasion
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- Shelby County Commissioner Edmund Ford Jr. was taken into federal custody after turning himself in Friday morning. He is charged with bribery and kickbacks concerning federal funds and six counts of attempted tax evasion, according to a federal indictment.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: Gene Hackman, wife Betsy Arakawa were dead for some time, found in different rooms, investigators say
item.description: A spokesperson for the state’s medical examiner declined to comment on whether any conclusions had been reached on the cause.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, ABC 4

item.title: Midwest winters are changing. So is the ancient sport of falconry
item.description: GREENLEAF, Wis. (AP) — Stephanie Stevens has a good reason to love the bone-numbing cold of a Wisconsin winter. Every weekend, she loads up her minivan with a large green box and drives out to rural areas, usually the edges of friends' farm fields.
item.dc:creator: MELINA WALLING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump hosts Zelenskyy at the White House and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico kids forced to live in 'underground caves,' court docs say
item.description: A New Mexico home where eight children lived with their two parents was described by state police as looking like "a city trash landfill.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, NewsNation

item.title: No new bombshells in Justice Department’s release of Jeffrey Epstein files
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Thursday released government documents related to wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein, but the first wave of files posted on the Justice Department website have largely been circulating in the public domain for years and didn't include any new bombshells.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Map: Listeria strain in deadly outbreak found as death toll climbs
item.description: An Indiana-based dairy company has recalled potentially affected shakes that were distributed nationwide to long-term care facilities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: GOP Sen. Mike Lee suggests letting ‘pirates’ take on cartels on the border
item.description: “Privateers is what they’re called,” he said. “Letters of marque and reprisal are authorized under Article 1, Section 8, Clause 10 of the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Landlord charged after allegedly leaving tenant without water for 54 days
item.description: The water was turned off from Dec. 6 until Jan. 2, as Yopp refused to have a plumber come by to fix it.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, NewsNation

item.title: Mexican National Guard on patrol in Tijuana keeping migrants, drugs out of US
item.description: Many of them have been placed at points where migrants and smugglers are known to cross the border, especially in the mountains east of the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Economic blackout protest underway Friday
item.description: (AP) — A grassroots organization is encouraging U.S. residents not to spend any money Friday as an act of “economic resistance” to protest what the group's founder sees as the malign influence of billionaires, big corporations and both major political parties on the lives of working Americans.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO and HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump plans tariffs on Mexico and Canada for Tuesday, while doubling existing 10% tariffs on China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump plans to impose tariffs on Canada and Mexico starting Tuesday, in addition to doubling the 10% universal tariff charged on imports from China.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FABIOLA SANCHEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Epstein files: AG Pam Bondi sends letter to Kash Patel accusing FBI of withholding docs
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi sent a fiery letter to FBI Director Kash Patel on Thursday accusing New York feds of withholding Jeffrey Epstein documents from her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look at Louisiana's Mardi Gras festivities through the years
item.description: After a weekslong pre-Lenten bash — complete with elaborate parades that meander through New Orleans, shimmery beads tossed from floats, streets lined with costumed revelers, lavish balls and seafood boils — Louisiana's 2025 Carnival Season is approaching its grand conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Freed Hamas captive tells harrowing story
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Trump administration sets the stage for large-scale federal worker layoffs in a new memo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government is facing a generational realignment as President Donald Trump directs federal agencies to develop plans for eliminating employee positions and consolidating programs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA cancels meeting to choose flu vaccine strain
item.description: The cancellation of the FDA meeting, alongside a similar postponement last week of a meeting of Centers for Disease Control and Prevention vaccine advisors, is adding to the growing concern among scientists and public health advocates that Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Texas lottery drawings that paid out big jackpots are the focus of widening investigations
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Two major lottery drawings in Texas that put nearly $180 million in the pockets of winning ticket holders have set off widening state investigations over concern that ticket sellers and buyers may have exploited the rules. The Texas Lottery, one of the largest in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gayle King, Katy Perry, Lauren Sánchez to board 'historic' Blue Origin space flight
item.description: "CBS Mornings" co-anchor Gayle King, singer Katy Perry and Lauren Sánchez will be among an all-woman crew blasting off on Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin as part of what's being called an "historic" space flight.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KRON4

item.title: USAID workers will be given 15 minutes to clear their workspaces as the agency gets dismantled
item.description: Thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development workers who have been fired or placed on leave as part of the Trump administration's dismantling of the agency are being given a brief window Thursday and Friday to clear out their workspaces.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Meet the only 2 passengers from the viral 'private' Southwest flight out of Utah
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- It's not every day that a commercial airplane is transformed into a "private" plane -- but two Utah students got to see what it's like when that does happen.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Agriculture secretary outlines plan to lower egg prices
item.description: “The Agriculture Department will invest up to $1 billion to curb this crisis and make eggs affordable again," Rollins wrote in an op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: iPhone bug that could suggest replacing 'racist' with 'Trump' is getting fixed
item.description: Apple is fixing a bug within the dictation feature on some iPhones that briefly suggests the word “Trump” when a word with an R consonant is spoken, including "racist.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
